evals.prompt: I have a microbiome OTU count table and sample IDs. I want Shannon alpha diversity per sample and a Bray-Curtis beta-diversity distance matrix between samples.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scikit-bio. Uses alpha_diversity('shannon', counts_matrix, ids=...) and beta_diversity('braycurtis', counts_matrix, ids=...) from skbio.diversity, noting counts must be integer abundances; alpha returns a Series and beta returns a DistanceMatrix.

assertions.value: Computes Shannon alpha diversity and a Bray-Curtis DistanceMatrix from integer counts.

evals.prompt: I have a UniFrac distance matrix and sample metadata with a treatment grouping. I want to ordinate the samples with PCoA and test whether the groups differ significantly.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scikit-bio. Runs pcoa(distance_matrix) from skbio.stats.ordination for the ordination (PC1/PC2 coordinates, proportion explained) and permanova(distance_matrix, grouping, permutations=999) from skbio.stats.distance to test group differences, returning the p-value.

assertions.value: Ordinates with PCoA and tests group separation with PERMANOVA permutation test.

evals.prompt: I want weighted UniFrac distances between my microbiome samples, which means I need to bring in my phylogenetic tree and map the feature IDs.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scikit-bio. Uses beta_diversity('weighted_unifrac', counts_matrix, ids=..., tree=tree, taxa=feature_ids), noting that phylogenetic metrics require the tree and feature (taxa) ID mapping; tree read via TreeNode.read.

assertions.value: Computes UniFrac passing a tree and taxa (feature) IDs alongside the counts.

evals.prompt: I have a DNA distance matrix for a set of taxa and want to build a neighbor-joining phylogenetic tree, then compute patristic distances between two tips.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scikit-bio. Uses nj(distance_matrix) from skbio.tree to build the neighbor-joining TreeNode, then computes patristic distance with tree.find('taxon1').distance(tree.find('taxon2')) (or cophenetic_matrix()).

assertions.value: Builds a neighbor-joining tree with nj() and computes patristic/cophenetic distances.

evals.prompt: I have a DNA FASTA. I want to reverse-complement it, transcribe to RNA, translate to protein, and find all ATG-initiated codon motifs with a regex.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scikit-bio. Reads with skbio.DNA.read, calls reverse_complement(), transcribe(), translate(), and uses find_with_regex for motif search, using the grammared DNA/RNA/Protein classes.

assertions.value: Uses skbio DNA/RNA/Protein methods (reverse_complement/transcribe/translate) and find_with_regex.

evals.prompt: I have a bulk RNA-seq counts matrix and want to find genes that are differentially expressed between two conditions with FDR correction.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-pydeseq2. The user wants negative-binomial differential expression testing on RNA-seq counts, not community diversity, ordination, or sequence/phylogenetic analysis.

evals.prompt: I already have a Newick tree and want to render a publication-quality annotated tree figure with colored clades and custom node styles.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-etetoolkit. The user wants rich tree visualization/annotation (ETE styling), whereas scikit-bio's tree support is construction/manipulation/metrics with only ASCII visualization.
